Fame.text: Your Fame total at the end of the game — the base of your score, before any Achievements or scenario bonuses.

Greatest Knowledge.text: 2 Fame per Spell in your deck, plus 1 Fame per Advanced Action in your deck.

Greatest Leader.text: The total level of all your Units. A wounded Unit only counts half its level, rounded down.

Greatest Adventurer.text: 2 Fame per Shield token you placed on an adventure site (dungeons, tombs, monster dens, ziggurats/pyramids, etc.).

Greatest Loot.text: 2 Fame per Artifact (in your deck or on a Unit), plus 1 Fame for every 2 crystals in your Inventory.

Greatest Conqueror.text: 2 Fame per Shield token you placed on a keep, mage tower, or monastery.

Greatest Beating.text: Minus 2 Fame per Wound card in your deck. Wounds attached to a Unit don't count.

Greatest Quester.text: 1 Fame per Quest Point you scored from completed Quest card steps (Apocalypse Dragon expansion).
Greatest Quester.source: Apocalypse Dragon Variant Rules, rulebook p.43

Cities Conquered.text: +10 Fame for each city you conquered this game. The default Solo Conquest setup uses a Level 5 city and a Level 8 city, but players may pick different levels to adjust difficulty.

All Cities Conquered.text: +15 Fame bonus if you conquered every city in the game — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.

Rounds Finished Early.text: +30 Fame for each Round you finished before the 6-Round limit — i.e. you beat the clock.

Dummy Player's Deck.text: +1 Fame for each card still unflipped in the Dummy Player's Deed deck at game end — a fuller deck means you finished with time to spare.

End of Round.text: +5 Fame if "End of the Round" had not yet been announced during your final Round.

City Revealed.text: +10 Fame if you revealed the map's capital city by the end of the game — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
City Revealed.source: First Reconnaissance scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.23

High Priestess Defeated.text: +20 Fame if you defeated the High Priestess — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
High Priestess Defeated.source: The Hidden Valley scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.26

Graveyards Sealed.text: +5 Fame for each Graveyard you sealed, plus +10 more if you sealed both Graveyards and also defeated the Necromancer.
Graveyards Sealed.source: The Realm of the Dead scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.25

Necromancer Defeated.text: +10 Fame if you defeated the Necromancer — sealing both Graveyards and defeating the Necromancer is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
Necromancer Defeated.source: The Realm of the Dead scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.25

For the Council: Quest Points.text: Your total Quest Points scored — the base of your score in this Quest-driven scenario. There's no Fame or Standard Achievements scoring in For the Council.
For the Council: Quest Points.source: For the Council scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.25

For the Council: Reputation.text: Tap the space your Shield token is actually on. You score (or lose) Quest Points equal to the modifier printed there — or −10 instead on an X space. Reputation +2 or higher (2 spaces past center) determines whether you Won or Lost.
For the Council: Reputation.source: For the Council scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.25

Round-Prep Offers.text: When a Dummy Player is in play, round-prep changes: the lowest Advanced Action offer card goes into the Dummy Player's deck (which is then reshuffled) instead of being discarded, and the lowest Spell offer card still goes to the bottom of the Spell deck as usual but also grants the Dummy Player one crystal of its color, with no 3-crystal cap.
Round-Prep Offers.source: Cooperative and Solo Scenarios, Dummy Player, rulebook p.15

Heads Defeated.text: +5 Fame for each Apocalypse Dragon head you defeated (excluding the Control head, which auto-defeats once the other 4 fall).
Heads Defeated.source: Against the Dragon scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.34

All Heads Defeated.text: +15 Fame more if you defeated all 4 heads — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost (the Control head auto-defeats once the other 4 fall).
All Heads Defeated.source: Against the Dragon scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.34

Horsemen Defeated.text: +4 Fame for each of the Four Horsemen you defeated.
Horsemen Defeated.source: Against the Horsemen scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.30

All Horsemen Defeated.text: +15 Fame more if you defeated all four Horsemen — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
All Horsemen Defeated.source: Against the Horsemen scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.30

Apocalypse is Here: Horsemen Defeated.text: +3 Fame for each of the Four Horsemen you defeated.
Apocalypse is Here: Horsemen Defeated.source: Apocalypse is Here scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.38

Avatars Defeated.text: +10 Fame for each Avatar of Tezla you defeated — Tezla's Spirit for the Elementalists, Dark Tezla for the Dark Crusaders.
Avatars Defeated.source: Life and Death scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.23

Both Avatars Defeated.text: +15 Fame more if you defeated both Avatars — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
Both Avatars Defeated.source: Life and Death scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.23

Relic Pieces Found.text: +5 Fame for each piece of the relic you found.
Relic Pieces Found.source: The Lost Relic scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.28

All Relic Pieces Found.text: +10 Fame more if you found every piece of the relic — this is also what determines whether you Won or Lost.
All Relic Pieces Found.source: The Lost Relic scoring, Solo, Shades of Tezla Scenario Book p.28

Destroyed Site Tokens.text: +3 Fame for each Destroyed Site token in your Inventory.
Destroyed Site Tokens.source: Against the Apocalypse scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.28

Ziggurat/Pyramid Floors Conquered.text: +5 Fame for each of the ziggurat and the pyramid you conquered at least one floor of — a flat bonus per site, not scaled by which exact floor you reached. Conquering a floor of both is also required to Win.
Ziggurat/Pyramid Floors Conquered.source: Against the Apocalypse scoring, Solo, Apocalypse Dragon rulebook p.28

Combat Level.text: How tough Volkare and the city are, chosen before setup — Daring, Heroic, or Legendary. This is also the base value (30/40/50) the Volkare Combat Bonus starts from if you defeat him.
Combat Level.source: Volkare's Quest / Volkare's Return, Scenario difficulty, Lost Legion rulebook p.17/p.14

Race Level.text: How much time pressure Volkare exerts, chosen before setup — Fair, Tight, or Thrilling. This is also the multiplier (1× / 1.5× / 2×) applied to the Volkare Combat Bonus if you defeat him.
Race Level.source: Volkare's Quest / Volkare's Return, Scenario difficulty, Lost Legion rulebook p.17/p.14

Volkare's Quest: Cities Conquered.text: +5 Fame for each city you conquered (Solo setup always has 2). Conquering cities isn't this scenario's goal, unlike Volkare's Return's +20 for its one city.
Volkare's Quest: Cities Conquered.source: Volkare's Quest scoring, Lost Legion rulebook p.17

City Conquered (Volkare's Return).text: +20 Fame if you conquered the city.
City Conquered (Volkare's Return).source: Volkare's Return scoring, Lost Legion rulebook p.14

Volkare Defeated.text: Whether Volkare's entire army was destroyed — this determines whether you Won or Lost, and whether the Volkare Combat Bonus applies at all.
Volkare Defeated.source: Volkare's Quest / Volkare's Return, Outcome, Lost Legion rulebook p.17/p.14

Volkare Combat Bonus.text: Only scored if Volkare was defeated: start from 30/40/50 Fame for Daring/Heroic/Legendary Combat Level, +2 Fame per card still left in Volkare's deck, then multiply that subtotal only by 1× / 1.5× / 2× for Fair/Tight/Thrilling Race Level.
Volkare Combat Bonus.source: Volkare's Quest / Volkare's Return scoring, Lost Legion rulebook p.17/p.14
